card.intuition: A powerful automation platform built into GitHub that lets you create custom workflows (CI/CD) triggered by events like push, pull request, or issue creation.
card.analogy: A smart home system for your code: if the front door opens (A push to main), turn on the lights (Run tests), start the oven (Build Docker), and notify the owner (Send Slack message).
when_to_use: Publishing NPM packages or Docker images automatically
Managing project boards and comments using bots
gotchas: Secrets Management: never put API keys in your `.github/workflows/*.yml` files. Use 'Secrets' in repository settings and access them via `${{ secrets.NAME }}`.
Caching: by default, every run starts from scratch. Use the `actions/cache` action to share `node_modules` or dependencies between runs to speed them up.

exercise.prompt: Identify the file format used to define GitHub Actions workflows.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.upper() == "YAML"
assert check("YAML")
print("PASS")
